model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Choose signal-only structured lifetime versus signal plus a public cancel operation.
source_of_truth: The executable DKR contract, saved generic Lite cancellation probe and result, current Lite types and scope source, and the preserved v1 checkpoint.
read_method: Run workers/dkr-2/replay.sh to pin every named hash, replay all cancellation cases, compare the saved result, and validate this checkpoint identity, fields, numeric walls, distinct refs, and blocked gate.
questions_answered: Current parent close cleans parent-owned resources before a live scalar child settles or closes.
Current parent close leaves active streams and three-level nested descendants running after parent resource cleanup.
Current scalar errors, stream completion, producer errors, and consumer break already propagate and clean their own invocation correctly.
A parent signal plus an optional caller signal can cancel one invocation without cancelling its sibling or parent.
Structured close can abort cooperative scalar descendants, terminate active streams, join nested descendants, and then clean parent resources.
The explicit context owner calls close, the invocation caller aborts its supplied signal, and running flows observe ctx.signal. A public cancel operation adds no caller.
Signal-driven scalar exits need core cancellation classification because the read-only prototype closes them as errors while current stream abort already closes as aborted.
No start, spawn, worker pool, session API, task handle, or general task registry is required.
questions_unanswered: A scalar promise that ignores its signal cannot be forced to stop. Structured close must wait for it, while adapters own bounded timeout policy.
The exact private descendant-settlement bookkeeping needs implementation review and must remain context lifecycle state rather than a public task registry.
Independent validation has not replayed the signal-only conclusion or the five active wall reads.
decision: candidate only: add ExecutionContext.signal, add optional signal to exec and execStream options, and make close abort descendants, return active stream iterators, join descendants, classify signal exits as aborted, then clean parent resources; do not add ExecutionContext.cancel, start, spawn, or task handles
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed and rerun DKR-2 when Lite source, the probe, an evidence hash, freshness, or independent review state changes

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Generic scalar, stream, nested, error, and targeted-signal probe
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay every generic case and confirm the candidate adds no session, cancel, start, spawn, worker-pool, or task surface.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Read-only baseline replay and unchanged current Lite source hashes
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay scalar and stream baselines and verify the pinned Lite source hashes without changing product files.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Writer checkpoint and missing independent validator record
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Require a separate validator to replay the conclusion and every wall before acceptance.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Authorized DKR-2 paths, named content hashes, saved replay, governed worker records, and store verification
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Resolve all six evidence hashes, compare DKR-2 writes with the packet allowlist, append through the absolute helper, and verify the run store.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V2 checkpoint with numeric walls, breached independence, pending judgement, and blocked downstream gate
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Validate complete identity, numeric walls, distinct refs, exact signal-only decision, rejection policy, and blocked downstream advance.

wall_gate.reason: The writer-only conclusion keeps single_llm_truth_acceptance_count at 1 until an independent validator replays the v2 checkpoint.

worked_checkpoint_trace.decision: rejected for downstream advance until independent validation

confidence_probability_update: 0.45 before generic probes; 0.91 after scalar, stream, nested-close, targeted-signal, cleanup-order, and unhandled-rejection replay
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Current parent close leaves scalar, stream, and nested descendants live after parent resource cleanup.
The signal-only contract gives each actual caller a control path without adding a second cancel surface.
Structured close needs private direct-descendant settlement accounting, not a public or schedulable task registry.
Signal-driven scalar exits need explicit aborted classification to match current stream cancellation semantics.
Existing scalar error, stream completion, producer error, and consumer break behavior must remain unchanged.
The candidate is generic Lite lifecycle behavior and carries no session-specific semantics.

candidate_pkrs.scope: Implement the accepted signal-only context and close semantics without public task or session surfaces.
Add generic scalar, stream, nested, targeted, cleanup-order, classification, and unhandled-rejection contract tests.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
